name: Block structure
description: The grouping of statements into a single unit, often allowing for nested scopes.

name: Garbage collection
description: Automatic memory management where the system reclaims memory no longer in use by the program.

name: Object-oriented programming
description: A paradigm based on the concept of 'objects' containing data and code.

name: Homoiconicity
description: The property that the program structure is represented as a data structure in the language itself.

name: Hindley-Milner type system
description: A type system that allows for powerful type inference without requiring explicit type annotations.

name: RAII
description: Resource Acquisition Is Initialization: a pattern where resource management is tied to object lifetime.

name: Lazy evaluation
description: The strategy of delaying the evaluation of an expression until its value is actually needed.

name: The Actor Model
description: A mathematical model of concurrent computation that treats 'actors' as universal primitives.

name: Ownership and Borrowing
description: A memory management model that uses strict rules to ensure memory safety without a garbage collector.

name: The Event Loop
description: A programming construct that waits for and dispatches events or messages in a program.

name: Relational Model
description: An approach to managing data using a structure and language consistent with first-order predicate logic.

name: Design by Contract
description: A software engineering method that defines the rights and obligations of software components through formal contracts.

name: Church-Turing Thesis
description: A fundamental hypothesis stating that any effectively calculable function can be computed by a Turing Machine.

name: Multiple Inheritance
description: A feature of object-oriented languages that allows a class to inherit from more than one parent class.

name: Object-Oriented Design
description: A software engineering method that models a system as a collection of interacting objects.

name: Metaprogramming
description: A technique where computer programs treat other programs as their data, allowing for code that writes or modifies code.

name: Borrow Checking
description: A feature of the Rust type system that ensures memory safety without a garbage collector by tracking ownership and lifetimes.
